title: Delightful Tunisian Quinoa Pilaf
description: A sweet and fluffy quinoa pilaf featuring a hint of Tunisian spice, ready to be devoured out of a hot stone bowl.
instructions: Heat the quinoa in a fine-mesh strainer over high heat, stirring constantly, until lightly toasted.
In a large saucepan, heat the vegetable broth over high heat and bring to a boil.
Add the quinoa to the saucepan and reduce the heat to low. Cover and cook for 15 minutes, or until the liquid has been absorbed.
While the quinoa cooks, heat the oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped onion and bell pepper to the skillet and cook, stirring occasionally, until tender.
Stir in the cumin, coriander, salt, and black pepper.
When the quinoa is done cooking, stir in the chopped onion and bell pepper mixture, pumpkin seeds, apricots, red food coloring, and lemon juice.
Transfer the quinoa mixture to a preheated stone bowl.
Serve immediately, garnished with additional pumpkin seeds and apricots if desired.
If desired, cook a few minutes longer in the stone bowl to create a crispy crust.
